data.title: US NFP: Hiring in December Slides Under Estimates with 50,000 New Jobs. Stocks Jump.
data.description:
Key points:
  • Nonfarm payrolls undershoot
  • December adds 50,000 jobs
  • Stocks open higher, dollar seesaws

Unemployment rate fell to 4.4% last month. Not too bad, not too good — just OK. Stocks were higher pre-market.

📉 Soft Jobs, Not a Meltdown

  • December payrolls printed at 50,000 new jobs, missing forecasts but fitting the “slow but not broken” theme that’s dominated the US labor market all year.
  • The unemployment rate dipped to 4.4%, telling us people aren’t getting fired en masse, even if companies aren’t exactly hiring with champagne and pizza either.

🧮 The Great Cooldown

  • Total job growth for 2025 came in at 584,000, a massive downshift from 2024’s two million jobs, confirming the Fed’s cooling campaign actually did its job.
  • Monthly hiring throughout the year averaged roughly 49,000, which is enough to keep the economy alive, but not enough to scare the Fed into slamming the brakes.

💵 Stocks Cheer, Dollar Throws a Fake

  • Futures jumped after the data, with traders betting softer hiring means lower rates are still on the menu, which is good news for stocks and risk assets.
  • The dollar initially slipped, then pulled a classic reversal, ripping back toward 158.00 as traders recalibrated their bets.
  • That whipsaw tells you one thing: this report was “Goldilocks” — not hot, not cold, but volatile enough to keep everyone guessing.

data.title: US 10Y Bond Yield Hits 4-week High
data.description:
US 10 Year Government Bond Yield increased to 4.21%, the highest since December 2025.

Over the past 4 weeks, US 10 Year Note Bond Yield gained 4.90 basis points, and in the last 12 months, it decreased 56.50 basis points.

data.title: Sysco Canada Named Atlantic Canada’s Best Places To Work 2026 by Atlantic Business Magazine!
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / January 9, 2026 / Sysco Corporation:

Originally published on January 1, 2026 on LinkedIn.

We're excited to announce that Sysco Canada has been named one of Atlantic Canada's Best Places to Work 2026 by Atlantic Business Magazine!

This award spotlights companies that excel in areas essential to exceptional workplaces: corporate culture, work/life balance, DEI, benefits/remuneration, incentives, and career development.

What sets us apart?

  • Flexible, hybrid work models offering the ideal balance of remote and in-office time

  • Competitive compensation, including annual base reviews tied to performance and corporate results

  • Recognition programs celebrating achievements with peer-nominated awards and travel/cash incentives

  • Investment in growth, including $2,500/year tuition reimbursement and tailored development plans

  • ESG impact, featuring route optimization to cut emissions and Canada's largest food recovery initiative

  • Comprehensive health coverage, including mental health support and virtual care

This award underscores our dedication to being an employer of choice in Atlantic Canada-prioritizing growth, balance, inclusion, and impact.

Interested in joining a Purpose-driven workplace where your well‑being and growth really matter? Explore opportunities here: sysco.ca/careers

View original content here.

About Sysco

Sysco is the global leader in selling, marketing and distributing food products to restaurants, healthcare and educational facilities, lodging establishments and other customers who prepare meals away from home. Its family of products also includes equipment and supplies for the foodservice and hospitality industries. With more than 76,000 colleagues, the company operates 340 distribution facilities worldwide and serves approximately 730,000 customer locations. For fiscal year 2024 that ended June 29, 2024, the company generated sales of more than $78 billion. Information about our Sustainability program, including Sysco's 2023 Sustainability Report and 2023 Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Report, can be found at www.sysco.com.

For more information, visit www.sysco.com or connect with Sysco on Facebook at www.facebook.com/SyscoFoods. For important news and information regarding Sysco, visit the Investor Relations section of the company's Internet home page at investors.sysco.com, which Sysco plans to use as a primary channel for publishing key information to its investors, some of which may contain material and previously non-public information. In addition, investors should continue to review our news releases and filings with the SEC. It is possible that the information we disclose through any of these channels of distribution could be deemed to be material information.

data.title: Japan PM Takaichi considering snap election in February, Yomiuri newspaper says
data.description:
Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is considering calling a snap election for parliament's lower house in the first half of February, the Yomiuri newspaper reported on Friday, citing political sources.

The move would allow Takaichi, Japan's first female prime minister, to take advantage of the strong approval ratings she's enjoyed since taking office in October. Her tough stance on China has appealed to right-wing voters but has fueled a major diplomatic dispute with the powerful Asian neighbour.

Takaichi's Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) is in coalition with the right-wing Ishin party. Their coalition is a few seats short of a majority in Japan’s lower house.

data.title: Canada Jobs Data Delivers Dose of Reality For Economy — Market Talk
data.description: Canada's jobs data covering December delivers a much-needed dose of reality about the state of the labor market, says Doug Porter, chief economist at BMO Capital Markets. Net job growth slowed sharply to 8,200, following an average gain of 60,000 in the preceding three months. More important, the unemployment rate rose sharply to 6.8%, undoing part of a 0.6-percentage-point drop in the prior three months. Porter says the 6.8% rate is above year-ago levels, "which, frankly, seems to jibe much better with reality." The report adds that hours worked fell 0.3% from the prior month, and the private sector shed workers. The October report won't push the Bank of Canada to mull rate cuts, Porter says, and it should quiet down talk about rate increases in H2. (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

data.title: Dow Gains After December Jobs Data — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

The Dow industrials rose after a mixed jobs report, which showed unemployment was lower than forecast, even as the economy added fewer roles than expected in December.

The U.S. gained 50,000 jobs last month, against consensus expectations of about 73,000 extra roles. Unemployment stood at stood at 4.4%, a touch lower than expected. Tallies for October and November were revised down by a combined 76,000 positions.

Up ahead, a meeting of the Supreme Court could potentially bring a ruling on President Trump's sweeping tariffs. Any decision could have big consequences for U.S. companies and for the government's ability to collect revenue from import levies.

The Nasdaq composite was little changed in morning trading, while the S&P 500 inched higher. Treasury yields wavered. Shares in Oklo and Vistra jumped after the two companies were tapped by Meta as part of the Facebook parent's sweeping nuclear-power plans.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Sysco Supports Local Farmers and Sustainable Agriculture Practices
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / January 9, 2026 / Sysco Corporation

Originally published on January 6th, 2025 on LinkedIn

Local fields. Local expertise. Local food.

For more than 30 years, Welgro Produce has been growing Brussels sprouts in Swords, with a third generation now carrying the family tradition forward. Harvested across more than 160 acres, their fresh Irish sprouts are delivered to customers within hours of picking.

Sysco is proud to partner with Welgro to support local growers, sustainable farming practices, and the producers who keep food close to home. Because when we invest in local farms, we strengthen local food systems and communities.

About Sysco

Sysco is the global leader in selling, marketing and distributing food products to restaurants, healthcare and educational facilities, lodging establishments and other customers who prepare meals away from home. Its family of products also includes equipment and supplies for the foodservice and hospitality industries. With more than 76,000 colleagues, the company operates 340 distribution facilities worldwide and serves approximately 730,000 customer locations. For fiscal year 2024 that ended June 29, 2024, the company generated sales of more than $78 billion. Information about our Sustainability program, including Sysco's 2023 Sustainability Report and 2023 Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Report, can be found at www.sysco.com.

For more information, visit www.sysco.com or connect with Sysco on Facebook at www.facebook.com/SyscoFoods. data.title: Silver Jumps After Soft US Payrolls
data.description:
Silver rose over 1.5% to above $78.2 per ounce on Friday as weaker US job creation reinforced expectations for Fed rate cuts, triggering renewed demand for precious metals after index driven selling pressure eased.

December nonfarm payrolls rose by just 50,000, well below forecasts, while the unemployment rate fell to 4.4%, confirming a low hiring, low firing labor market that aligns with an outlook for lower interest rates rather than renewed tightening.

That shift reduced real yield pressure and encouraged fresh long positioning and short covering in silver futures once mechanical selling linked to annual commodity index rebalancing began to subside.

Geopolitical risks around Venezuela and East Asia provided secondary support, but the rebound was chiefly driven by the repricing of US rates, with thin liquidity amplifying the upside move after two sessions of sharp liquidation.

data.title: Argus Research lifts Reddit's PT
data.description:
** Argus Research raises PT for RDDT to $300 from $250, implying 18.1% upside to stock's last close

** Brokerage expects RDDT's aggressive spending on R&D to generate growth in average daily users as co gains share in large and fragmented advertising world

** "We believe RDDT's growth runway is promising, justifying the premium, at least in the near term and during a bull market," Argus Research says

** 18 of 27 brokerages recommend "buy" or higher, 8 "hold" and 1 "sell"; stock has median PT of $245 - LSEG-compiled data

** RDDT had gained 40.6% in 2025

data.title: PNDORA: Q4 growth missed guidance, but margins held firm as focus shifts to innovation and cost control
data.description:
Q4 organic growth was 4%, with full-year growth below guidance due to softer North American holiday traffic and stable but mixed European performance. Profitability remained strong, supported by cost discipline and consistent marketing investment, while strategic focus shifts to product innovation and margin protection amid commodity headwinds.
